When the city widened Main Street, officials negotiated with the Okafor family, purchased the strip of land in front of their bakery at appraised value, and recorded the payment before construction began.
When the city widened Main Street, crews demolished the front section of the Okafor family's bakery without notice, and officials told the family no payment would be made for the land.
The Ridgefield city council voted to acquire three parcels for a stormwater project, held condemnation hearings for each owner, and deposited court-determined compensation before taking possession.
The Ridgefield city council voted to acquire three parcels for a stormwater project, canceled the scheduled condemnation hearings, and took possession before any owner received payment.
The Marbury zoning board reviewed Dale Hutchins's permit for a workshop on his own lot, applied the published setback rules, and approved the plans that met the code.
The Marbury zoning board reviewed Dale Hutchins's permit for a workshop on his own lot, then ordered the half-built structure removed under a rule adopted after construction started.
State inspectors cited Herrera Trucking for a paperwork lapse, allowed the company to correct its filings within thirty days, and returned the impounded rigs once the fine was paid.
State inspectors cited Herrera Trucking for a paperwork lapse, then auctioned the company's three impounded rigs the following week before any hearing on the citation was held.
When Granite Supply stopped delivering under its signed agreement, the county court heard both parties, enforced the contract's terms, and ordered the deliveries completed at the agreed price.

When Granite Supply stopped delivering under its signed agreement, the county court declared the contract void by administrative order, and the buyer's claim was closed without either party being heard.
After the diocese fell behind on a utility bill, the town placed a standard lien on the parish hall, and the congregation kept the building while paying the balance on a schedule.
After the diocese fell behind on a utility bill, the town seized the parish hall outright, transferred the deed to itself, and the congregation was locked out the same month.
Milton State University leased its research farm to the Beckers under a ten-year contract, and when administrators wanted the land back early, they paid the buyout amount written into the lease.
Milton State University leased its research farm to the Beckers under a ten-year contract, and when administrators wanted the land back early, they canceled the lease by memo and changed the locks.
The harbor commission renewed the Tran family's dock permit under the posted fee schedule, and their boats kept the slips they had held for eleven seasons.
The harbor commission reassigned the Tran family's slips to a new operator midway through their paid permit term, and their boats were towed from the dock they had held for eleven seasons.
The regional power utility routed a transmission line across the Nakamura ranch after negotiating an easement, recording it with the county, and paying the appraised annual fee.
The regional power utility routed a transmission line across the Nakamura ranch without an easement, and crews cleared a strip of pasture before the family learned of the project.
The Fairview school district needed land for a new elementary school, bought the Priddy parcel at a price set by two independent appraisals, and closed the sale with the owners' signatures.
The Fairview school district needed land for a new elementary school, fenced off the Priddy parcel before any purchase, and began grading while the owners' objection sat unanswered.
When a gas company sought the minerals under Cora Whitfield's pasture, the state land office confirmed her recorded mineral rights, and drilling proceeded only after she signed a royalty agreement.

When a gas company sought the minerals under Cora Whitfield's pasture, the state land office reassigned her recorded mineral rights to the company by directive, and drilling began without her signature.
The housing authority converted the Delmar apartment block to public units by purchasing the building from its owner, Ruth Calloway, at a negotiated price recorded at closing.
The housing authority converted the Delmar apartment block to public units by declaring the deed of its owner, Ruth Calloway, transferred to the city, with no purchase and no payment recorded.
After a tax office error overstated Ostrowski Hardware's bill, the county corrected the assessment, refunded the overpayment with interest, and released the lien on the store within the month.
After a tax office error overstated Ostrowski Hardware's bill, the county foreclosed on the store, sold it at auction, and kept the proceeds while the owner's correction request went unread.
The county airport authority raised hangar rents at renewal, applied the new rate only to leases signed afterward, and honored the remaining terms of existing tenants at the old rate.
The county airport authority raised hangar rents at renewal, billed existing tenants the new rate back three years, and padlocked the hangars of pilots who declined to pay the retroactive charge.

The public employees' retirement system recalculated benefits under a new formula, applied the change to future contributions only, and left balances members had already accrued in their accounts untouched.
The public employees' retirement system recalculated benefits under a new formula, applied the change to past years, and withdrew funds members had already accrued from their individual accounts.

When a public university laboratory used Amos Reddick's patented valve design, administrators signed a licensing contract with him, and royalty payments arrived each quarter as the agreement specified.

When a public university laboratory used Amos Reddick's patented valve design, administrators declared the patent unenforceable against university projects, and his licensing contract was canceled without payment.
During the drought, the water district cut deliveries to the Salinas orchard according to the priority dates on its recorded rights, and the family received the allocation those rights specified.
During the drought, the water district canceled the Salinas orchard's recorded rights by resolution, and the family's allocation was redirected to other users without a hearing or compensation.
The county health department revoked Mireille Fontaine's food-cart license after a violation, but she kept ownership of the cart and equipment and sold them to another vendor at market price.

The county health department revoked Mireille Fontaine's food-cart license after a violation, and inspectors also confiscated the cart and equipment she owned, disposing of them without a forfeiture proceeding.
The regional transit authority fell behind on payments to Calder Paving, then followed the contract's dispute clause, submitted to arbitration, and paid the balance the arbitrator set.
The regional transit authority fell behind on payments to Calder Paving, then adopted a resolution declaring the contract void, and the company's invoices were returned unpaid.

State prison rules cap beards at half an inch so photographs and searches stay reliable. Inmate Aaron Feld's faith directs him not to trim his beard at all. Guards note that contraband has been found in beards twice this year. The warden must set a policy that covers both.

The Al-Noor mosque requests a variance to broadcast the dawn call to prayer over outdoor speakers, as the Methodist church's bells already ring at noon. The homes nearest the mosque sit forty feet away, and the town noise ordinance bars amplified sound before seven. What should the variance board do?

During a measles outbreak with nineteen confirmed cases, the county health office reviews forty religious exemption requests from families at two schools. Excluding unvaccinated children would keep them out of class for six weeks; admitting them may extend the outbreak. The statute permits either reading.

The only pharmacy in Garrow County is staffed by a pharmacist whose faith bars him from dispensing one prescribed medication. The next pharmacy is sixty miles away, and the state board is weighing whether his license requires him to fill every lawful prescription or allows a referral system.

A Santería congregation conducts animal sacrifice at a home on a quarter-acre lot, following its rites for disposal. Neighbors report odors and runoff to the health department, which applies the same carcass rules it applies to farms. The congregation says no other site is within reach.

The night-shift emergency room at Doyle Memorial has two technicians: one observes a Friday-sundown sabbath, the other a Sunday observance, and both have requested those days off. The schedule requires one of them each weekend. The hospital must post next quarter's rotation by Monday. Whose observance yields?

A Good Friday procession has walked the length of Camden Avenue for decades, closing the street for three hours. The avenue now carries the only bus routes to the regional hospital and two factories with shift changes that afternoon. The transit agency asks the parish to reroute; the parish says the route is the rite.

Doctors at Kestrel Children's Hospital say a nine-year-old needs a transfusion within days; her parents decline it, following their faith's teaching, and offer consent for every other treatment. The hospital can petition a judge to override them. The parents remain her caregivers through whatever follows. What should the judge weigh?

A growing Orthodox congregation in the Weld Street historic district needs a larger sanctuary, but district rules cap building heights below what its liturgical design requires. The same rules have blocked a hotel and two office proposals. The congregation applies for an exception the district has never granted.

Radford High recognizes student clubs that admit any enrolled student. A scripture study club applies, with a bylaw requiring officers to sign the club's statement of faith. Membership stays open to all, but offices do not. The activities office must apply one recognition policy to every club.
Clerk Naomi Adler asked the county recorder's office to schedule her off on Saturdays for sabbath observance. The office swapped her weekend rotation with a coworker, and she kept her position.

Clerk Naomi Adler asked the county recorder's office to schedule her off on Saturdays for sabbath observance. The office denied any change to her weekend rotation and dismissed her after two absences.
An inmate at Fairview state prison requested halal meals on religious grounds. The kitchen added him to the religious diet list, and his trays arrived prepared accordingly.
An inmate at Fairview state prison requested halal meals on religious grounds. The kitchen refused to add him to the religious diet list and served him the standard trays.

A congregation applied to convert a vacant supermarket on Route 9 into a mosque. The planning board applied the same parking and occupancy standards used for other assembly uses and approved the application.

A congregation applied to convert a vacant supermarket on Route 9 into a mosque. The planning board added parking and occupancy conditions it required of no other assembly use and denied the application.

A student group at Kingsley Middle School met at the flagpole before class for prayer. The principal let the gathering continue, and students who walked past without joining faced no questions from staff.
A student group at Kingsley Middle School met at the flagpole before class for prayer. The principal ordered the gathering to disband and assigned detention to the students who had joined it.
Witness Tomás Ibarra told the court he could not swear a religious oath. The judge administered a secular affirmation, and his testimony proceeded that morning.
Witness Tomás Ibarra told the court he could not swear a religious oath. The judge refused any alternative wording and struck his testimony from the record.
Firefighter Harjit Singh asked to wear a turban with his dress uniform at department ceremonies. The chief approved the accommodation, and he stood with his company at the fall parade.
Firefighter Harjit Singh asked to wear a turban with his dress uniform at department ceremonies. The chief denied the request and removed him from the roster for the fall parade.
Council member-elect Vera Osei declined to place her hand on a scripture at the swearing-in. The clerk administered the oath without it, and she took her seat that evening.
Council member-elect Vera Osei declined to place her hand on a scripture at the swearing-in. The clerk refused to administer the oath without it, and her seat was left vacant.
The family of a patient at Mercer County Hospital asked that a Buddhist monk perform bedside rites. The hospital added the monk to the visitor list and provided a quiet hour.
The family of a patient at Mercer County Hospital asked that a Buddhist monk perform bedside rites. The hospital kept the monk off the visitor list while admitting the staff chaplain.
Warehouse worker Eli Brandt lost his job after declining Saturday shifts for religious observance. The state benefits office reviewed his file and approved his unemployment claim.
Warehouse worker Eli Brandt lost his job after declining Saturday shifts for religious observance. The state benefits office classified the refusal as misconduct and denied his unemployment claim.
A Pentecostal congregation without a building applied to hold Sunday services in the Elmwood park pavilion. The parks department issued the permit on the same terms as other groups' reservations.

A Pentecostal congregation without a building applied to hold Sunday services in the Elmwood park pavilion. The parks department turned down the application because the gathering was a worship service.
At the county license office, Amina Diallo asked to keep her headscarf on for her driver's license photo. The examiner photographed her with the scarf, and the license printed that day.
At the county license office, Amina Diallo asked to keep her headscarf on for her driver's license photo. The examiner required her to remove it or leave without a license.
A Hindu student association at Corliss State applied for recognized-organization status and meeting space. The student affairs office granted the status under the same rules as other groups.

A Hindu student association at Corliss State applied for recognized-organization status and meeting space. The student affairs office rejected the application, stating that religious groups could not receive recognition.

Police recruit Dawit Bekele requested a beard exemption from the academy's grooming code on religious grounds. The academy granted the exemption it already gave for medical reasons, and he continued training.

Police recruit Dawit Bekele requested a beard exemption from the academy's grooming code on religious grounds. The academy, which granted medical exemptions, denied his request and dropped him from training.
The Harbor Street shelter, operating under a city contract, held an evening chapel service. Residents who skipped the service kept their beds and their places in the meal line.
The Harbor Street shelter, operating under a city contract, held an evening chapel service. Residents who skipped the service lost their beds and were moved to the back of the meal line.
During Miriam Katz's interview for a district librarian position, the panel asked about cataloging systems and reading programs. She received an offer based on the posted scoring rubric.
During Miriam Katz's interview for a district librarian position, the panel asked which church she attended. Her application was marked incomplete when she declined to answer, and no offer came.

A Jewish burial society asked the county cemetery to schedule an interment within twenty-four hours, per religious practice. The cemetery rearranged its crew schedule and completed the burial on time.

A Jewish burial society asked the county cemetery to schedule an interment within twenty-four hours, per religious practice. The cemetery held the burial to its standard five-day queue despite open slots.
Machine operator Yusuf Kamara asked his plant supervisor for two short prayer breaks per shift. The supervisor adjusted his relief schedule, and production records showed his line met its quota.
Machine operator Yusuf Kamara asked his plant supervisor for two short prayer breaks per shift. The supervisor refused any adjustment and wrote him up each time he stepped away.
In a custody review, Judge Palmer asked both parents about school schedules and medical care. The order she issued said nothing about either household's religious practice.
In a custody review, Judge Palmer asked both parents about their church attendance. The order she issued granted weekend custody on the condition that the children be taken to services.
A Quran study circle applied to reserve a meeting room at the Bellamy branch library. The library booked the room under its standard community-use policy, and the circle met on Tuesdays.
A Quran study circle applied to reserve a meeting room at the Bellamy branch library. The library refused the booking, stating that rooms were closed to religious study groups.
The Ralston County assessor opened staff meetings with a prayer and told employees attendance during it was optional. Analyst Kurt Weber stepped out each time and received his scheduled promotion.
The Ralston County assessor opened staff meetings with a prayer and required employees to remain standing for it. Analyst Kurt Weber stepped out each time and was passed over for promotion.

A hurricane closed the roads to Camden County's records office two days before the statutory deadline for permit renewals. The county administrator can let forty businesses lapse under the written rule or extend the deadline by memo, a power no statute grants her. Which choice should she make?

The Belmore prosecutor has evidence to charge an eighty-year-old caretaker under a statute that mandates prison for medication errors. Filing the charge follows the law as written; declining it spares a defendant most jurors would not convict, but makes one office the judge of which statutes count. What should the prosecutor file?

A nineteen-twenties ordinance still on Halvern's books bars food sales from carts, and the city has ignored it for a decade while a street-vendor economy grew. A restaurant owner now demands enforcement against her competitors. Enforcing revives a rule everyone abandoned; not enforcing leaves the written code and the practiced law apart. What should the city do?

Detective Moray believes a kidnapped child is inside a warehouse, and the on-call judge is unreachable for another hour. Entering now could save the child; the search statute requires a warrant, and evidence from a warrantless entry may free the suspect later. She stands at the door deciding.

During the outbreak, Governor Ashe's health orders closed businesses statewide for six months under a statute written for seventy-two-hour emergencies. Legislators could not convene safely to extend the authority. Lives may depend on orders continuing; the words of the statute have run out. Should the orders remain in force?

Treston's tax office proposes forgiving penalties for residents who pay back taxes this year. The amnesty would recover millions the city cannot otherwise collect, while residents who paid on time under the written schedule receive nothing. Collections and consistency point in opposite directions. Should the office run the program?

An analyst at the state auditor's office copied confidential files to expose a contracting scheme, violating the records statute while revealing violations of the bidding law. Charging her enforces the statute she broke; declining signals that some breaches serve the code's purpose. The auditor must recommend one course.

Judge Farrow must sentence a first-time courier to the fifteen-year minimum the statute prescribes, a term three times what she gave a ringleader last year under an older law. Following the statute produces the disparity; departing from it substitutes her judgment for the legislature's text. The hearing is Monday.

After a balcony collapse, Kenwick's council wants to require immediate steel reinforcements in older apartment buildings, applying the standard to structures approved under earlier codes. Owners built to the rules in force at the time; tenants live under balconies the new engineering deems unsound. How far back should the requirement reach?

Flood water is rising toward Parville's electrical substation, and the only available contractor wants a no-bid contract signed tonight. The procurement statute requires two weeks of public bidding for work this size. The city engineer estimates the substation floods in four days. Which document should the manager sign?
When the city audit found Mayor Trellis had used a municipal credit card for personal travel, she repaid the funds and the ethics board processed her case under the same ordinance applied to clerks.
When the city audit found Mayor Trellis had used a municipal credit card for personal travel, her office ordered the ethics board to close the file and the ordinance was applied only to clerks.

After Officer Danby's blood test showed alcohol following an on-duty crash, the Weston police department charged him under the same statute used for civilian drivers and suspended his license per the code.

After Officer Danby's blood test showed alcohol following an on-duty crash, the Weston police department logged the incident as equipment failure and no charge was filed under the statute used for civilian drivers.
The state fisheries agency announced new catch limits in March, applied them beginning in June as the statute required, and licensed boats operating under the old limits in April kept their permits.
The state fisheries agency announced new catch limits in March and revoked the permits of boats that had fished under the old limits in February, applying the rule to conduct before its adoption.

County Treasurer Voss missed the property tax deadline on her lakeside cabin, and the office added the ten percent penalty her account generated automatically, the same charge mailed to every late filer.
County Treasurer Voss missed the property tax deadline on her lakeside cabin, and a deputy deleted the ten percent penalty from her account while every other late filer received the standard charge.
The Colton building department approved the senator's home addition after the plans passed the same load calculations and two inspections that the code requires for every residential project.
The Colton building department stamped the senator's home addition approved without the load calculations or the two inspections that the code requires for every residential project.

The Selby city council posted the landfill contract seventy-two hours before the vote and took public comment, following each step the open-meetings statute sets out for contracts above one hundred thousand dollars.

The Selby city council approved the landfill contract during an unannounced session at a member's home, skipping the posting and comment steps the open-meetings statute sets out for contracts above one hundred thousand dollars.
District Attorney Rankin turned over the lab report that weakened her case against the defendant, filing it with the court within the ten days the disclosure statute allows.
District Attorney Rankin kept the lab report that weakened her case against the defendant in a desk drawer through the trial, past the ten-day deadline the disclosure statute sets.
Sheriff Okafor served the eviction order on his own cousin's hardware store on the date the court set, following the same notice steps his deputies used at every other property that month.
Sheriff Okafor left the eviction order for his cousin's hardware store unserved for eleven months past the date the court set, while his deputies served every other property on schedule.
The state environmental agency fined Ketterman Chemical the per-day amount the statute fixes for discharge violations, calculating the total from the plant's own monitoring logs as the rule directs.
The state environmental agency reduced Ketterman Chemical's discharge fine to a tenth of the per-day amount the statute fixes, after the director met privately with the company's lobbyist.
The state legislature passed a records law requiring agencies to answer document requests within twenty days, and it applied the deadline to its own chambers as well as to the executive departments.

The state legislature passed a records law requiring agencies to answer document requests within twenty days, and it wrote its own chambers out of the deadline while binding the executive departments.

Superintendent Lake submitted her travel reimbursements with receipts through the district's claim system, and the business office reviewed them under the same policy that covers teachers' conference expenses.
Superintendent Lake paid herself travel reimbursements without receipts by signing her own claim forms, bypassing the review policy that the business office applies to teachers' conference expenses.
Judge Hemsley disclosed that her husband owned shares in the plaintiff company and transferred the case to another judge, following the recusal rule in the state judicial code.
Judge Hemsley kept the case after learning her husband owned shares in the plaintiff company, ruling for the plaintiff without the disclosure the state judicial code's recusal rule requires.
The state cosmetology board revoked licenses only after the hearings its regulations describe, and each stylist cited last year received notice, a hearing date, and a written decision.

The state cosmetology board revoked four stylists' licenses by phone call without the notice, hearing, or written decision its regulations describe, while other cited stylists received the full process.
The Harker County board awarded the bridge repair contract through the sealed-bid process state law mandates, opening all five envelopes at the public meeting and selecting the lowest qualified bid.
The Harker County board awarded the bridge repair contract to the chairman's former business partner before the bid deadline passed, leaving the five sealed envelopes state law mandates unopened.
Governor Reyes ended the drought emergency order after the thirty days the statute permits, and her office asked the legislature to renew the water restrictions through a bill.
Governor Reyes extended the drought emergency order for a second year past the thirty days the statute permits, renewing the water restrictions by her signature alone each month.
The Calder health department closed the Riverside Cafe after two failed inspections, the threshold its written code sets, and reopened it once the reinspection the code requires was passed.
The Calder health department closed the Riverside Cafe after a single complaint call, though its written code sets a two-failed-inspection threshold, and set no reinspection date for reopening.
The Marden police department issued march permits to every group that met the ordinance's route and insurance requirements, approving applications in the order they arrived.
The Marden police department denied a march permit to a group that met the ordinance's route and insurance requirements, citing no provision and returning the application unsigned.
Pemberton State's president followed the faculty handbook when dismissing a tenured professor, convening the review committee and presenting the documented cause the handbook's procedure requires.
Pemberton State's president dismissed a tenured professor by emailed letter over a weekend, convening no review committee and citing none of the causes the faculty handbook's procedure requires.

The Ellsworth parking bureau dismissed tickets only through the written appeal process, and the clerk's log showed every dismissal last quarter matched to a filed appeal and a hearing officer's decision.
The Ellsworth parking bureau's clerk dismissed two hundred tickets last quarter with no filed appeals or hearing decisions, clearing them for callers who reached her direct line.

The secretary of state's office rejected every candidate filing that arrived after the statutory five o'clock deadline, including the filing submitted by the secretary's own party's incumbent at five-twenty.

The secretary of state's office accepted a five-twenty filing from the secretary's own party's incumbent while rejecting other candidates' filings that arrived after the statutory five o'clock deadline.

A federal judge has ordered Corvin County's jail to release inmates by Friday to cure overcrowding, and the county board says compliance requires releasing people mid-sentence. The sheriff answers to the board but is also an officer of the court. Whose instruction should the sheriff follow on Friday?

A dam upstream of Selwick is failing, and evacuating requires spending the legislature has not authorized. Lawmakers cannot assemble for nine days; the governor can act tonight by claiming a power the constitution assigns to the assembly. The water rises either way. What should the governor sign?

The state supreme court must review the pipeline permit, which turns on soil chemistry none of the justices have studied. Deferring to the agency's scientists respects expertise the court lacks; reviewing the data itself keeps the agency from judging its own permit. How closely should the court look?

Halvorsen's legislature wants authority to cancel any agency regulation by simple resolution, arguing that unelected staff now write most binding rules. Agencies answer that rulemaking only fills in details lawmakers delegated. The amendment would return power to the elected body while letting it reach into execution. Should it pass?

A trial judge has ordered Mayor Quill to reinstate a police chief fired for falsifying reports, and the city's lawyers believe the order misreads the charter. Obeying keeps the chief in command during the appeal; refusing sets the executive above a court's judgment. The reinstatement deadline is noon tomorrow.

A new synthetic drug is hospitalizing teenagers in Brandt County weekly. The health director can ban the compound today under a broad reading of her inspection powers, or wait four months for the legislature to schedule it by statute. Waiting risks more hospitalizations; acting stretches her statute. Which should she take?

The governor's security office has intelligence about a threat to the capitol and wants to screen legislators' visitors, searching bags at the chamber doors. The senate president objects that the executive would control access to the legislature itself. The session opens in three days. Who should run the checkpoint?

Varn's council must cut the budget, and the municipal court's caseload has fallen by half. Trimming the court's funding matches every other department's cut; the judges answer that a council setting their budget case-by-case could pressure their rulings. The vote is next month.

The assembly's ethics committee has subpoenaed the governor's scheduling records to trace a contractor's visits. The governor's counsel says advisers cannot speak candidly if every meeting becomes public; the committee says it cannot investigate what it cannot see. A court date would take a year. Should the records be produced?

The adoption statute in Merion says nothing about children born through surrogacy, and a family court judge has such a case today. Ruling creates a rule the legislature never wrote; dismissing leaves the child without legal parents until a bill someday passes. What should the judge order?
When the legislature declined to fund the new patrol aircraft, Governor Merrit's office withdrew the purchase order and submitted the request again in the next budget session.
When the legislature declined to fund the new patrol aircraft, Governor Merrit's office bought it anyway by moving money from accounts the appropriations act assigned to bridge repairs.
After the state supreme court struck down the licensing fee, the revenue department stopped collecting it within a week and mailed refunds as the ruling directed.
After the state supreme court struck down the licensing fee, the revenue department kept collecting it for two years and issued none of the refunds the ruling directed.
While the water-rights lawsuit was before the district court, the legislature waited for the ruling, then wrote a statute applying the court's standard to future permit disputes.
While the water-rights lawsuit was before the district court, the legislature passed a statute directing the court to enter judgment for the irrigation district by name.
After the Norbury council passed the sign ordinance over his veto, Mayor Casteel directed city departments to enforce it, filing his objections in the public record.
After the Norbury council passed the sign ordinance over his veto, Mayor Casteel instructed city departments to disregard it and enforced his original version by executive memo.
When the assembly's oversight committee subpoenaed the commerce department's contract files, the agency delivered the documents by the return date and its director testified at the hearing.
When the assembly's oversight committee subpoenaed the commerce department's contract files, the agency shredded the documents that week and its director declined to appear at the hearing.
The Linden school board set the district's grading policy by vote, and Superintendent Paz implemented it in every school while reporting her disagreement at the public meeting.
The Linden school board set the district's grading policy by vote, but Superintendent Paz issued a different policy to principals and instructed them to ignore the board's version.
Ordered by the appeals court to release the prison medical records, the corrections department produced them in ten days while filing its objections through a further appeal.
Ordered by the appeals court to release the prison medical records, the corrections department announced it would not comply and continued withholding the files after losing its further appeal.
The Farley County executive submitted her pick for public works director to the county board, and the appointment took effect after the confirmation vote the charter requires.
The Farley County executive installed her pick as public works director the day the county board voted down the appointment, titling him acting director to skip the confirmation the charter requires.
After the state courts struck down the districting map, the legislature drew a new map under the ruling's criteria and funded the judiciary at the level the budget formula produced.
After the state courts struck down the districting map, the legislature kept the map in place for the election and cut the judiciary's budget by a third the following week.
Asked to expand the housing tax credit, the state finance agency replied that the statute capped eligibility, and it forwarded the proposal to the legislature for amendment.
Asked to expand the housing tax credit, the state finance agency rewrote the eligibility cap by internal rule, granting credits the statute did not authorize while the legislature was out of session.
Judge Ollan ruled that the transit statute's plain words excluded ferry service, writing that any expansion of the program's coverage would have to come from the assembly.
Judge Ollan ordered the transit agency to add ferry service under a statute that listed only buses and rail, setting fare schedules and route maps from the bench.
Governor Stancik asked the legislature for a special session to authorize the quarantine extension, and lawmakers passed the enabling bill before the original order expired.
Governor Stancik extended the quarantine by decree after the legislature voted down the enabling bill, announcing that his orders would substitute for the statute lawmakers had declined to pass.
The Weldon city charter assigns hiring to the city manager, and council members forwarded resident complaints about the parks crew to her office without directing any dismissals.
Though the Weldon city charter assigns hiring to the city manager, three council members summoned the parks crew's supervisor and ordered him to dismiss two groundskeepers by Friday.
When the state auditor opened a review of the highway fund, the transportation department turned over its ledgers within the week and scheduled staff interviews the audit statute provides for.
When the state auditor opened a review of the highway fund, the transportation department locked the ledgers in a records room and barred auditors from the building.

When a vacancy opened on the municipal bench, County Executive Hale nominated a magistrate and waited to administer the oath until the county board held the confirmation vote the charter assigns to it.
When a vacancy opened on the municipal bench, County Executive Hale administered the oath to his nominee the same afternoon, bypassing the confirmation vote the charter assigns to the county board.
The Dorval council appropriated funds for the crossing guards program, and Mayor Ibsen's administration spent the money on guard salaries as the line item specified.
The Dorval council appropriated funds for the crossing guards program, but Mayor Ibsen's administration left the line item unspent and shifted the money to his downtown plaza project.
Under the court's injunction limiting encampment sweeps, the Granton police department rewrote its patrol orders to match the ruling's notice requirements and logged each cleanup for the monitor.
Despite the court's injunction limiting encampment sweeps, the Granton police department continued clearances without the required notices and stopped filing cleanup logs with the monitor.
When merchants appealed their citations from the street-fair ordinance, the Kelton council let the municipal court hear the cases and amended the ordinance for the following year.
When merchants appealed their citations from the street-fair ordinance, the Kelton council voted to decide the cases itself and entered judgments before the municipal court could hear them.
The state treasurer held the disaster grant checks until the assembly passed the appropriation bill, then released the funds to the counties the morning after the governor signed it.

The state treasurer wired disaster grant funds to the counties while the appropriation bill was still in committee, spending from the treasury without a passed appropriation or the governor's signature.

Judge Toller denied the petition to overturn the parole board's decision, writing that the statute placed release timing in the board's hands and the record showed the board had followed its procedures.
Judge Toller replaced the parole board's decision with his own release schedule for every inmate in the county, though the statute placed release timing in the board's hands.

A records request seeks the case file on the Harmon warehouse fire while detectives are still interviewing witnesses. The open records act presumes release, and the family of an injured worker wants the findings; investigators say publishing statements now would let suspects align their accounts. The custodian has ten days to respond.

Parents have asked the Greeley school district to release the disciplinary file of a teacher who was transferred between schools twice, and the teachers association says personnel records are closed under the contract. The superintendent can release a summary, the full file, or nothing while the grievance is pending. Which records should the district open?

An engineering study of the Norquist dam maps the failure points that a flood or an attack could exploit, and downstream homeowners have requested it to assess their risk. Releasing the full study would publish the vulnerabilities; releasing a summary would leave residents relying on the district's own characterization. The board meets in closed session Tuesday.

The charter review commission wants to draft options in private workshops, saying members float ideas they would not put their names to in public, then present finished proposals at open hearings. The open meetings statute covers deliberations of public bodies, and two newspapers have asked to attend the workshops. The commission votes on its own rules first.

The audit that exposed billing irregularities at the county garage quotes interviews that could identify the mechanic who first reported them, and he asked for confidentiality when he came forward. A records request now seeks the complete audit. Redacting the interviews would remove the evidence supporting the findings. What should the auditor release?

The winning bid for the county's ambulance contract includes staffing models the company calls trade secrets, and the losing bidder has requested the full proposal to check the scoring. Disclosure law exempts proprietary data but requires enough release to explain the award. The purchasing director must decide how much of the proposal the public needs to verify the award.

After a lunchroom injury at Corbett Middle School, parents asked for the incident report, which names the students involved, all of them minors. Student privacy law closes those names; the injured child's family says the sequence of adult responses is what they want examined. The district must respond to the request by Friday.

The city is negotiating to buy flood-prone lots along Miller Creek, and publishing its appraisal ceiling now would tell every seller the maximum the city will pay. The council's practice is to disclose real estate terms after closing; a neighborhood group argues residents deciding whether to sell need the figures beforehand. Closed session is scheduled Thursday.

A breach of the county's payment system exposed vendor bank data, and the recovery contract was signed under an emergency exception without public bids. Security consultants advise withholding the breach details until patches are complete; vendors want to know what was taken and when. The commissioners must set a disclosure date at the next session.

A donor offered Landis State University forty million dollars for a new engineering hall on condition of permanent anonymity, and the university's gift policy requires publishing donor names above ten thousand dollars. The gift would fund two hundred scholarships; the policy exists so the public can see who funds campus buildings. The regents meet next month.
Millhaven's budget office posted the full line-item spending file on the city website each quarter, and residents could download every department's figures the day the books closed.
Millhaven's budget office stopped posting the line-item spending file, and residents who asked for department figures were told the records were internal working papers not subject to release.
The Calloway school board debated the school closure list in open session, posted the agenda three days ahead, and published the vote tally with each member's name that evening.
The Calloway school board settled the school closure list in a private session held before the posted meeting, and the public agenda that evening showed only a single ratification vote.

Reporter Ana Delgado requested the state agriculture department's inspection reports, and the records office delivered the complete set within the statutory twenty days, with fees itemized on the invoice.

Reporter Ana Delgado requested the state agriculture department's inspection reports, and the records office responded after seven months with a fee estimate of eighteen thousand dollars and no documents.
Before the vote on the Ridgeway subdivision, zoning commissioner Ed Paulsen disclosed that his brother owned an adjacent parcel and recused himself, and the disclosure was entered in the minutes.
Before the vote on the Ridgeway subdivision, zoning commissioner Ed Paulsen said nothing about his brother's adjacent parcel, cast the deciding vote, and the minutes recorded no disclosure.
The Brookfield water utility mailed customers the lead test results for all sampling sites in June, and the full laboratory data was posted on the utility's site the same week.
The Brookfield water utility received lead test results for all sampling sites in June, filed them without notice to customers, and the laboratory data was withheld when residents asked.
Kessler County awarded the jail food contract after posting all four bids online, and the selection memo explaining the scoring was released with the award notice.
Kessler County awarded the jail food contract without posting the four bids, and the selection memo explaining the scoring was marked confidential when a losing bidder requested it.
After the pursuit on Route 9, the Danby police department released the incident report and dashboard video within the ten days its policy sets, with officers' names included.
After the pursuit on Route 9, the Danby police department denied requests for the incident report, and the dashboard video was recorded over before the review board convened.
Ferris State University's foundation published its donor-funded travel ledger each year, and trustees answered questions about the president's trips at the annual open budget forum on campus.

Ferris State University's foundation moved the president's travel spending into an account outside the published ledger, and trustees declined every question about the trips at the annual budget forum.
The Hale County court clerk kept the settlement in the bridge collapse suit in the public docket, and copies were available at the counter for the standard page fee.
The Hale County court clerk sealed the settlement in the bridge collapse suit without a sealing order in the file, and counter staff told requesters no such case existed.
The public hospital board discussed the emergency room closure at a noticed meeting, took comment from staff and patients, and issued a written statement of the reasons for its decision.
The public hospital board decided the emergency room closure by email poll among members, and no notice, comment period, or statement of reasons accompanied the announcement.

The Tolliver County election office posted precinct-level results with the number of rejected ballots and the reason codes, and observers from both campaigns watched the canvass from the counting floor.
The Tolliver County election office posted county totals only, withheld the rejected-ballot counts and reason codes, and observers from both campaigns were kept outside the counting floor.
The teachers' pension fund listed each private investment, its fees, and its yearly return in the annual report, and the investment committee's minutes were archived on the fund's website.
The teachers' pension fund reported private investments as a single combined line without fees or returns, and the investment committee's minutes were deleted under a two-year destruction schedule.
The state highway division published every change order on the Route 12 project as it was signed, with the added cost and the engineer's written justification attached.
The state highway division signed forty change orders on the Route 12 project without publishing them, and the added costs appeared only after the final invoice was paid.
The Adair County health department posted every restaurant inspection score in a searchable database, and the underlying inspector notes were released to anyone who filed a request.
The Adair County health department removed the restaurant inspection database from its website, and requests for the inspector notes were answered with pages blacked out except the dates.
The Senate finance committee marked up the tax bill in a session streamed online, and each amendment's sponsor and vote count appeared in the posted transcript by morning.
The Senate finance committee marked up the tax bill in a session closed to cameras, and the transcript was published with the amendment sponsors' names and vote counts omitted.
Before trading the lakefront parcel to a developer, the Ellsworth parks board released the appraisals, held two evening hearings, and published the trade terms a month before signing.
Before trading the lakefront parcel to a developer, the Ellsworth parks board withheld the appraisals, scheduled no hearings, and the trade terms became public only after the deed was recorded.
The Bexley sheriff's office itemized its seized-asset fund in the county's annual audit, and the auditor's questions about equipment purchases were answered in a public exit conference.
The Bexley sheriff's office kept its seized-asset fund out of the county's annual audit, and the auditor's questions about equipment purchases were returned unanswered for the third year.

Ferndale's city attorney filed monthly statements of outside counsel fees with the clerk, and the invoices for the annexation litigation were open for inspection with privileged lines marked and logged.
Ferndale's city attorney stopped filing statements of outside counsel fees with the clerk, and the invoices for the annexation litigation were shredded after the case settled.
The gas utility's rate increase request went before the commission with the cost studies attached to the public docket, and hearing dates were printed in customers' bills.
The gas utility's rate increase was approved in a commission session that appeared on no calendar, and the cost studies were stamped proprietary and left out of the docket.
Mayor Corliss published her calendar of meetings with the stadium developers, and the term sheet under negotiation was posted for comment before the council took it up.
Mayor Corliss met the stadium developers eleven times without calendar entries, and the term sheet reached the council the morning of the vote with no posting for comment.
Every Saturday morning, the Okonkwo family wiped the baseboards, vacuumed under the sofa cushions, and returned each toy in the living room to its labeled bin before lunch.
Every Saturday morning, the Okonkwo family stepped around the toys spread across the living room floor, left the week's crumbs under the sofa cushions, and stacked more laundry on the stairs.
At the close of each shift at Tavolo, the line cooks scrubbed the flat-top, changed the fryer oil on schedule, and mopped the floor drains before locking up.
At the close of each shift at Tavolo, the line cooks left the flat-top crusted with the day's cooking, ran the fryer oil weeks past its change date, and let the floor drains clog.
At Meridian Design, Priya cleared her desk each evening, filed loose papers into labeled trays, and rinsed her mug in the kitchenette before heading home.
At Meridian Design, Priya left stacks of papers spreading onto the next desk, kept four unwashed mugs growing rings on her monitor stand, and dropped wrappers beside the full bin.
The superintendent at Gable Court swept the lobby twice daily, replaced burned-out stairwell bulbs the day they failed, and hosed the trash room down every Friday.
The superintendent at Gable Court let leaves and flyers gather in the lobby, left stairwell bulbs dark for months, and let bags pile in the trash room until the corridor smelled of it.
Before opening Crescent Bakery each day, Hugo wiped flour from every shelf, washed the display glass inside and out, and brushed crumbs from the proofing racks into the compost pail.

Before opening Crescent Bakery each day, Hugo left flour drifted along the shelves, opened with fingerprints layered across the display glass, and let crumbs sit on the proofing racks from the week before.
At the Rowan Freight warehouse, crews swept each aisle at shift change, coiled the air hoses onto wall hooks, and wiped hydraulic drips from the floor before the next pallet run.
At the Rowan Freight warehouse, crews left broken strapping across the aisles at shift change, dropped air hoses where they finished, and let hydraulic drips spread into a film across the floor.
In Room 12 at Alder Elementary, the students wiped the paint tables after art, stacked the chairs at the final bell, and emptied the pencil sharpener tray into the bin each Friday.
In Room 12 at Alder Elementary, the students left paint hardening on the tables after art, went home with chairs scattered, and let the pencil sharpener tray overflow onto the shelf.
Breaking camp at Twin Pines, the Herrera brothers packed out every wrapper, scattered the cold ashes from the fire ring, and left the tent pad raked as they had found it.
Breaking camp at Twin Pines, the Herrera brothers left wrappers pressed into the mud, abandoned half-burned logs and cans in the fire ring, and drove off with food scraps strewn across the tent pad.
After each charter, the crew of the Tern rinsed salt from the deck hardware, dried and stowed the life vests, and pumped and washed the bilge before the next group boarded.
After each charter, the crew of the Tern left salt crusting the deck hardware, heaped damp life vests in the cabin, and let bait water stand in the bilge until the next group boarded.
At Ironbark Fitness, members wiped each bench after use, returned dumbbells to the rack in order, and the staff laundered the towel supply and mopped the mats every night.
At Ironbark Fitness, members left sweat pooled on the benches, abandoned dumbbells across the floor, and the staff let used towels mound in the corner while the mats went unmopped for weeks.
Between clients at the Juniper salon, Renata swept the cut hair into the bin, wiped the chair and counter, and set her combs into the sanitizing jar.
Between clients at the Juniper salon, Renata let cut hair drift across the floor, left dye streaks drying on the chair and counter, and used the same combs uncleaned all day.
After rehearsal, the members of Glasshouse coiled their cables, carried empty bottles out to the recycling, and vacuumed the practice room carpet before switching off the lights.
After rehearsal, the members of Glasshouse left cables tangled across the floor, added their bottles to the rows along the wall, and ground chip crumbs into the practice room carpet.
In the dorm room they shared, Callie washed her dishes the same evening, kept her laundry in a lidded hamper, and wiped the shared mini-fridge shelf every Sunday.
In the dorm room they shared, Callie stacked unwashed dishes on the windowsill for days, spread laundry across both beds, and left a spill hardening on the shared mini-fridge shelf.
At the end of each day in his woodshop, Gerrit swept the sawdust into the collector, oiled the plane bodies, and hung every chisel back on its outlined peg.
At the end of each day in his woodshop, Gerrit left sawdust banked against the benches, let rust spot the plane bodies, and buried his chisels under offcuts on the table.
Between seatings at Larkspur, the servers brushed crumbs from every banquette, polished the water glasses against the light, and reset each table with straightened linens.
Between seatings at Larkspur, the servers seated new parties onto crumb-strewn banquettes, set out water glasses with lip marks, and left the linens stained from the earlier tables.
At the Bramble Veterinary clinic, technicians mopped each exam room between patients, laundered the kennel blankets daily, and wiped the waiting-room chairs down every hour.

At the Bramble Veterinary clinic, technicians moved patients into exam rooms still tracked with fur and paw prints, reused kennel blankets for days, and left the waiting-room chairs unwiped between visits.
After Saturday's match, the Brackenfield squad hung their kits to dry, hosed the mud from their boots at the outside tap, and swept the locker room floor before leaving.
After Saturday's match, the Brackenfield squad left damp kits balled in the lockers, tracked mud from their boots down the corridor, and stepped over the tape and cups on the locker room floor.
At the Spin Cycle laundromat, the attendant cleared lint traps every hour, wiped detergent spills from the folding tables, and mopped the entryway whenever rain tracked in.
At the Spin Cycle laundromat, the attendant let lint mat inside the traps for weeks, left detergent spills gumming the folding tables, and let tracked-in rain dry into streaks across the entryway.
Behind the Nakamura house, Kenji deadheaded the flower beds weekly, cleared the gutters each fall, and repainted the fence pickets the summer the finish began to peel.
Behind the Nakamura house, Kenji let the flower beds fill with bindweed, left the gutters packed until water ran down the siding, and let the fence pickets gray and split unpainted.
After the late showing at the Orpheum cinema, the ushers swept popcorn from every row, wiped the cup holders, and cleaned the lobby glass before the doors were locked.
After the late showing at the Orpheum cinema, the ushers locked up with popcorn drifted under every row, soda dried in the cup holders, and handprints layered across the lobby glass.
In her joinery workshop, Greta cut each dovetail by hand, tested the drawer's slide a dozen times, and rubbed three coats of oil into the walnut before delivery.
In her joinery workshop, Greta stapled the drawer corners instead of cutting joints, skipped the slide test, and sent the walnut out with one thin coat of oil.
On the Hartwell kitchen job, tiler Beno measured the wall twice, cut every edge piece to fit the window casing, and the grout lines ran true from counter to ceiling.
On the Hartwell kitchen job, tiler Beno set the tiles without measuring the wall, filled the gaps at the window casing with extra grout, and the lines wandered from counter to ceiling.
At the pass of Chez Aveline, cook Matteo plated each order to the ticket, wiped every rim, and sent back his own sauce twice to reduce it to the house standard.
At the pass of Chez Aveline, cook Matteo plated orders without reading the tickets, left thumbprints on the rims, and sent out sauce that had not finished reducing.
Rebuilding the vintage coupe's brakes, mechanic Ines torqued each caliper bolt to the manual's figure, bled the lines twice, and road-tested the car before handing back the keys.
Rebuilding the vintage coupe's brakes, mechanic Ines tightened the caliper bolts by feel, skipped bleeding the lines, and handed back the keys without a road test.
Tailor Behnam basted the suit jacket for two fittings, matched the pinstripes across every seam, and finished the buttonholes by hand before the wedding date.
Tailor Behnam skipped both fittings for the suit jacket, let the pinstripes mismatch at every seam, and machine-punched the buttonholes the morning of the wedding date.
In the pottery studio, Ume wedged her clay until the air pockets were gone, trimmed each bowl's foot on the wheel, and fired the set twice to the glaze chart.
In the pottery studio, Ume threw her bowls from unwedged clay, left the feet untrimmed, and pulled the set from a single short firing with the glaze bubbled.
At the Kestrel boatyard, shipwright Aldo faired the hull planks until his palm slid smoothly along them, sealed every fastener, and the skiff took no water on launch day.
At the Kestrel boatyard, shipwright Aldo left the hull planks unfaired with proud fastener heads unsealed, and the skiff took water at three seams on launch day.
At the print shop, Vera pulled test sheets until the ink density matched the client's proof, aligned the registration marks, and boxed five hundred flyers with even margins.
At the print shop, Vera ran the job without pulling a test sheet, and the five hundred boxed flyers went out with the registration marks doubled and the margins cut into the text.
Baker Solveig weighed her flour and water to the gram, folded the dough on the hour through the morning, and scored each loaf so the crust split along its seam.
Baker Solveig scooped her flour and water without weighing, skipped the morning folds, and the unscored loaves burst at random spots along their sides in the oven.
Hanging the nursery shelves, Priyanka found the studs, leveled each bracket with a spirit level, and loaded the boards with books to test them before the baby came home.
Hanging the nursery shelves, Priyanka screwed the brackets into bare drywall by eye, and the boards pulled loose under their first stack of books before the baby came home.
Cobbler Anton stitched the new soles along the original channel, replaced both heel counters, and buffed the leather so the boots left his shop ready for another decade.
Cobbler Anton glued the new soles over the worn ones, left the collapsed heel counters in place, and the boots came apart at the toe within a month of leaving his shop.
In the fabrication shop, welder Paz ground each joint clean before striking an arc, ran even beads along the trailer frame, and checked every weld against her square.
In the fabrication shop, welder Paz struck her arcs over mill scale and paint, left gaps in the beads along the trailer frame, and skipped the check against her square.
For his shop class final, Devon sanded his cutting board through four grits, eased every edge, and applied the mineral oil in thin coats over three evenings.
For his shop class final, Devon sanded his cutting board with a single coarse grit, left the saw marks and sharp corners, and flooded on the mineral oil the night before.
Reroofing the Calloway barn, the Herrera crew snapped chalk lines for every course, drove four nails per shingle, and flashed the chimney so the loft stayed dry through the storm season.
Reroofing the Calloway barn, the Herrera crew laid the courses by eye, drove two nails per shingle, and left the chimney unflashed. The loft dripped through the first storm of the season.
Luthier Mirek seasoned the spruce top for two years, tuned the braces by tapping and shaving, and set the guitar's action so every fret rang clear to the twelfth.
Luthier Mirek built with spruce that had dried for two weeks, glued the braces in unshaved, and shipped the guitar with strings buzzing on the first five frets.
At the frame shop, Noor cut her miters a hair long and shaved them to a seamless corner, mounted the watercolor on acid-free board, and dusted the glass before sealing the back.
At the frame shop, Noor nailed her miters with open gaps at each corner, taped the watercolor straight onto cardboard, and sealed a thumbprint and two hairs under the glass.
Watchmaker Pell disassembled the pocket watch under a loupe, cleaned each pivot, replaced the worn mainspring, and regulated the movement across six positions before returning it.
Watchmaker Pell oiled the pocket watch through the open case back without disassembly, left the worn mainspring in place, and returned it running eleven minutes fast a day.
Fencing the north pasture, Amos tamped each post in gravel, stretched the wire with a come-along until it sang, and the line held square through the spring thaw.
Fencing the north pasture, Amos set the posts in loose backfill, stretched the wire by hand, and the line sagged and leaned within a month of the spring thaw.
At the bindery, Selin sewed the journal's signatures onto linen tapes, nipped the spine rounds evenly, and pared the leather so the cover closed without a gap.
At the bindery, Selin glued the journal's signatures straight into the spine, skipped rounding it, and left the leather unpared so the cover gapped open on the table.
Painting the grocer's window sign, Ansel snapped guide lines, drew the letters twice in chalk before loading his brush, and the gold leaf edges met the outlines without a waver.
Painting the grocer's window sign, Ansel freehanded the letters without guide lines or chalk, and the gold leaf strayed past the outlines while drips dried down the glass.
Every evening, Mara cooked a dinner of grilled fish, brown rice, and steamed vegetables for her family, and they finished the meal with a bowl of sliced fruit.
Every evening, Mara microwaved a dinner of frozen pizza and fries for her family, and they finished the meal with a second helping of ice cream from the tub.
At his desk in the accounting office, Dev unpacked a lunch of lentil soup, whole-grain bread, and an apple, and he refilled his water bottle twice before the afternoon meetings.
At his desk in the accounting office, Dev worked through lunch without eating, then emptied a bag of chips and two candy bars from the vending machine before the afternoon meetings.
In the school cafeteria, twelve-year-old Priya chose the tray with baked chicken, salad, and milk, and she ate the pear her mother had packed during the afternoon break.
In the school cafeteria, twelve-year-old Priya skipped the lunch line entirely, and she spent her money on soda and a sleeve of cookies from the snack cart during the afternoon break.
After her evening workout at the gym, Tessa ate the chicken wrap and banana she had packed, and she was in bed with a glass of water by ten.
After her evening workout at the gym, Tessa drove straight to a burger stand, ordered a double combo with a milkshake, and finished a bag of fries in the parking lot.
On the construction site, foreman Ruiz opened his cooler at noon and ate turkey sandwiches on wheat bread with carrots and an orange, drinking water through the shift.
On the construction site, foreman Ruiz skipped his noon break again and ate nothing until dusk, when he bought two hot dogs and an energy drink from a roadside cart.
During her night shift at the hospital, nurse Adaeze ate the quinoa bowl with roasted vegetables she had brought from home, then snacked on almonds between rounds.
During her night shift at the hospital, nurse Adaeze ate nothing until three in the morning, then finished a box of donuts from the break room between rounds.
At the farmhouse table, Old Henrik ate oatmeal with berries each dawn before milking, and his midday meal was vegetable stew made from the kitchen garden.
At the farmhouse table, Old Henrik drank only coffee each dawn before milking, and his midday meal was a packet of snack cakes eaten standing at the counter.
At the harbor restaurant on Friday nights, the Okafor family ordered grilled snapper with greens and shared one dessert among the four of them before walking home.
At the harbor restaurant on Friday nights, the Okafor family ordered fried platters with extra cheese sauce, and each of the four finished a separate dessert before driving home.
Between shifts at the department store, cashier Lena ate a spinach salad with grilled chicken in the break room and kept a bag of walnuts in her locker for later.
Between shifts at the department store, cashier Lena skipped lunch in the break room again and kept a bag of gummy candy in her locker, finishing it by closing time.
At the summer music festival, Jonas sought out the stall selling grain bowls with roasted vegetables, ate one for dinner, and carried a refillable water flask between stages.
At the summer music festival, Jonas ate funnel cake for dinner and two more before midnight, and he drank only soda from the stall between stages.
At the auto repair garage, mechanic Sofia stopped at noon for the rice, beans, and salad she packed each morning, eating at the workbench she had cleared.
At the auto repair garage, mechanic Sofia worked through noon without stopping, then ate a gas-station hot dog and a package of mini donuts at the workbench.
On the overnight shift at the shipping warehouse, Marcus ate the tuna sandwich and orange from his lunchbox at two, and he drank water instead of the free soda.
On the overnight shift at the shipping warehouse, Marcus brought no lunchbox and ate nothing until two, then drank three free sodas and a bag of cheese puffs.
In her dorm kitchen during exam week, Amara cooked pots of vegetable curry with brown rice each night and kept cut fruit in the shared refrigerator for studying.
In her dorm kitchen during exam week, Amara lived on instant noodles and energy drinks each night, and she skipped breakfast every morning to keep studying.
Aboard the fishing boat Petrel, deckhand Iver ate the oat porridge the cook made at dawn and a supper of baked cod with potatoes after the nets came in.
Aboard the fishing boat Petrel, deckhand Iver skipped the porridge the cook made at dawn and ate a supper of chocolate bars and cola after the nets came in.
Traveling for work, Nadia ordered the grilled vegetable plate at the hotel restaurant, ate fruit from the lobby bowl at breakfast, and left the minibar chocolates unopened.
Traveling for work, Nadia skipped breakfast and dinner at the hotel restaurant, and by midnight she had emptied the minibar of its chocolates, chips, and cookies.
At the call center, Tomás kept a container of hummus and cut peppers beside his headset and ate a lentil salad in the courtyard on his lunch break.
At the call center, Tomás kept a two-liter soda and a tub of licorice beside his headset and stayed on calls through his lunch break without eating a meal.
On the film set, gaffer Bea filled her plate at the catering table with roast chicken, greens, and fruit, and she ate at each scheduled meal break.
On the film set, gaffer Bea passed the catering table without taking a plate, worked through both scheduled meal breaks, and dined on pastries from the coffee cart at wrap.
On Sunday afternoons, Grandpa Emil simmered a pot of bean and vegetable soup, portioned it into jars for the week, and set out apples on the counter.
On Sunday afternoons, Grandpa Emil stacked frozen dinners and boxed pastries in the freezer for the week, and the fruit bowl on the counter sat empty again.
At the bakery where she worked, Ruta ate the yogurt, muesli, and berries she brought from home each morning and saved the leftover pastries for the neighbor's chickens.
At the bakery where she worked, Ruta ate leftover frosted pastries for breakfast each morning and again at closing, and the lunch she brought from home stayed in her bag.
On his overnight haul, trucker Dale stopped at the diner outside Larkin and ordered the grilled chicken plate with side salad, refilling his thermos with water for the road.
On his overnight haul, trucker Dale drove past the diner outside Larkin without stopping to eat, then filled the cab with chips, jerky, and cola at the fuel pumps.
Every workday at the insurance office, Devika climbed the six flights to her floor, and at lunch she walked the river loop before returning to her desk.
Every workday at the insurance office, Devika rode the elevator to her floor, and at lunch she stayed seated at her desk scrolling her phone until meetings resumed.
Before breakfast each morning, Mr. Halvorsen did thirty minutes of stretching and push-ups in his living room, then biked to the market for the day's groceries.
Before breakfast each morning, Mr. Halvorsen settled into his recliner in the living room, and he drove the three blocks to the market for the day's groceries.
Camille used her membership at the neighborhood gym three evenings a week through the winter for rowing and weights, and by spring she completed the full circuit without resting.
Camille's membership at the neighborhood gym went unused through the winter, and by spring she was winded after carrying a laundry basket up a single flight of stairs.
At Brookfield Middle School, gym teacher Ono ran every lap he assigned his students, and he joined the staff soccer match each Friday after classes.
At Brookfield Middle School, gym teacher Ono watched from a folding chair while his students ran laps, and he skipped the staff soccer match each Friday after classes.
On the Kettleson farm, Ingrid hauled feed sacks and walked the fence lines each morning, and at sixty she still stacked the season's hay bales herself.
On the Kettleson farm, Ingrid drove the truck even between nearby barns and hired out every lifting task, and at sixty she could no longer stack a single hay bale.
After his knee surgery at Mercer Hospital, Silas completed the exercises his physical therapist listed every morning, and within eight weeks he walked the ward without the cane.
After his knee surgery at Mercer Hospital, Silas left the exercise sheet from his physical therapist untouched in a drawer, and after eight weeks he still needed the cane for the ward hallway.
Each Saturday at Alder Park, Wren met her running group at the fountain for an eight-kilometer loop, then finished with stretches on the grass by the bandstand.
Each Saturday at Alder Park, Wren waved to her running group from a bench by the fountain, then spent the morning seated by the bandstand with her phone.
Between shifts at the distribution warehouse, Kofi ran the loading dock stairs and did pull-ups on the rack frame, and he lifted the heaviest cartons to the top shelf unaided.
Between shifts at the distribution warehouse, Kofi sat in the break room through every pause in the schedule, and he asked a coworker whenever a carton needed lifting to the top shelf.
On her sales trips, Maren packed running shoes and used each hotel's pool and fitness room before breakfast, logging laps while the lobby coffee brewed.
On her sales trips, Maren left her running shoes at home and passed the hotel's pool and fitness room each morning, watching television in bed until breakfast ended.
At the garden supply store, clerk Dario carried the forty-pound soil bags to customers' cars two at a time, and after closing he cycled the six miles home.
At the garden supply store, clerk Dario waved customers toward the soil bags rather than carry them, and after closing he waited by the curb for a ride to cover the six miles home.
At the lakeside festival, Beatriz entered the morning five-kilometer fun run and finished it, then spent the afternoon dancing in front of the main stage.
At the lakeside festival, Beatriz skipped the morning five-kilometer fun run she had registered for, then spent the afternoon in a folding chair far from the main stage.
In his fourth-floor walk-up, Elio took the stairs with his grocery bags in one trip, and on weekends he helped neighbors carry furniture up the landings.
In his fourth-floor walk-up, Elio paused at every landing with his grocery bags and split them into three trips, and on weekends he declined when neighbors carried furniture upstairs.
At the community pool each dawn, retired welder Gus swam forty laps before the lanes filled, and he toweled off in time to open his shop by eight.
At the community pool each dawn, retired welder Gus sat in the shallow end for a few minutes without swimming a lap, then rested on the deck chairs until the lanes filled.
On the Saddleback trail, the Varga sisters hiked the full ridge route with loaded packs, reaching the summit cairn by noon and descending before the storm clouds gathered.
On the Saddleback trail, the Varga sisters turned back at the first switchback, breathing hard under light daypacks, and waited in the car while the storm clouds gathered.
In his home garage, Oscar hung a pull-up bar between the joists and trained there each evening, and he pushed his stalled car to the curb alone that spring.
In his home garage, Oscar stored boxes on the pull-up bar he had hung between the joists, and that spring three neighbors pushed his stalled car to the curb for him.
On the Milton Street construction site, apprentice Faye carried her share of the cinder blocks up the scaffold each day and climbed the ladders without pausing on the rungs.
On the Milton Street construction site, apprentice Faye passed her share of the cinder blocks to others each day and stopped to catch her breath at every ladder landing.
At the dog run on Cypress Lane, Hana jogged alongside her retriever for the whole hour, throwing the ball from the far fence and racing him back.
At the dog run on Cypress Lane, Hana stayed on the bench the whole hour, tossing the ball a few feet while her retriever circled the fence alone.
Rain or shine, Soren cycled the seven kilometers to the ferry terminal, stood on the crossing, and cycled the hill on the far side to reach the print shop.
Rain or shine, Soren drove the seven kilometers to the ferry terminal, sat in his car on the crossing, and drove the hill on the far side to the print shop.
At the Birchwood senior center, eighty-year-old Rosa led the twice-weekly stretching class and walked the garden path circuit ten times each visit with her walking poles.
At the Birchwood senior center, eighty-year-old Rosa signed up for the twice-weekly stretching class but sat by the window each session, and the garden path circuit went unwalked.
On their beach vacation, the Lindqvist family swam past the sandbar each morning and played volleyball until the vendors packed up, walking the shoreline back to the cottage.
On their beach vacation, the Lindqvist family stayed under the umbrella each morning with tablets and magazines, and they drove the four hundred meters of shoreline back to the cottage.
At the Alvarez family dinner, Marisol waited until her brother finished describing his week before she spoke, then asked him two questions about the trip he had mentioned.
At the Alvarez family dinner, Marisol cut in before her brother finished describing his week, talked over his reply, and turned to her phone while he was still speaking.
When the server at Harlan's Grill brought the wrong dish, Priya thanked her for checking, explained the mix-up quietly, and waited while the kitchen prepared the plate she had ordered.
When the server at Harlan's Grill brought the wrong dish, Priya snapped her fingers at her, repeated the order in a raised voice, and pushed the plate back across the table.
During the Monday planning meeting, Devon let Ines finish presenting her schedule, took notes on her points, and asked whether she wanted feedback now or after the session.
During the Monday planning meeting, Devon interrupted Ines three times while she presented her schedule, answered emails as she spoke, and left the room before she reached her final point.
At the counter of Willow Street Bakery, Tomas greeted the customer ahead of him, held the door for a woman carrying boxes, and told the cashier to take her time with the register.

At the counter of Willow Street Bakery, Tomas stepped in front of the customer ahead of him, let the door swing back on a woman carrying boxes, and drummed on the register while the cashier counted change.
On the crowded morning ferry, Lena offered her seat to a passenger holding a toddler, moved her bag from the bench, and lowered her voice on the phone call.
On the crowded morning ferry, Lena spread her bag across two seats while a passenger holding a toddler stood nearby, and carried on her phone call at full volume.
In Ms. Okafor's art class, Ruben raised his hand and waited to be called on, then thanked a classmate who lent him a brush before the period ended.
In Ms. Okafor's art class, Ruben shouted answers while other students were speaking, grabbed a brush from a classmate's desk without asking, and returned it without a word.
When Mr. Feld moved into the Larchmont apartments, he introduced himself to the neighbors on his floor, kept his music low after ten, and answered the doorman's questions with a smile.
When Mr. Feld moved into the Larchmont apartments, he walked past the neighbors on his floor without a greeting, played music past midnight, and waved off the doorman's questions mid-sentence.
After the volleyball match, team captain Sana shook hands with each opposing player, waited for the referee to finish his notes, and let the visiting team use the water station first.
