After the volleyball match, team captain Sana walked past the opposing players' extended hands, talked over the referee as he finished his notes, and blocked the visiting team from the water station.
At the garage rehearsal, drummer Cole waited for the singer to finish her verse before suggesting a change, and asked the bassist whether the new tempo worked for him.
At the garage rehearsal, drummer Cole broke in mid-verse to demand a change, rolled his eyes while the singer answered, and told the bassist his opinion on tempo didn't matter.
At the lakeside campsite, Iris asked the family at the next pitch before borrowing their mallet, returned it within the hour, and kept her lantern dimmed after they turned in.
At the lakeside campsite, Iris took the mallet from the next pitch without asking, kept it overnight, and shone her lantern toward the family's tent after they had turned in.
Aboard the charter boat Persimmon, deckhand Milo greeted each guest by name, listened to the whole question before answering, and stepped aside so an older passenger could reach the rail.
Aboard the charter boat Persimmon, deckhand Milo answered guests' questions with one-word replies while looking past them, and shouldered ahead of an older passenger to reach the rail first.
In the Delford warehouse breakroom, Otis asked whether the seat was taken before sitting, offered his coworker half the table, and waited for the microwave until she had finished.
In the Delford warehouse breakroom, Otis pushed his coworker's lunch aside to claim the table, and pulled her bowl from the microwave mid-cycle to heat his own food first.
At the Marigold salon, Bree arrived ten minutes early, told the stylist she could wait while the previous client finished, and thanked her by name on the way out.
At the Marigold salon, Bree arrived twenty minutes late, told the stylist to hurry while the previous client was still in the chair, and left without a word when finished.
In the aisles of Fenwick Books, Arjun lowered his voice on his call, stepped back so another shopper could reach the shelf, and asked the clerk if she had a moment before posing his question.
In the aisles of Fenwick Books, Arjun carried on his call at full volume, stood blocking the shelf while another shopper waited, and cut into the clerk's conversation to demand directions.
At the Ridgeline gym, Petra asked how many sets the man at the squat rack had left, worked in between his rounds when he offered, and re-racked the plates for the next person.
At the Ridgeline gym, Petra pulled the plates off the squat rack without asking the man who was mid-set, and walked away leaving the bar loaded for the next person.
At Corliss Hardware, Wendell held his questions until the clerk finished with the customer ahead, said please and thank you at the register, and returned his cart to the rack outside.
At Corliss Hardware, Wendell broke into the clerk's exchange with the customer ahead, tossed his money on the counter without a word, and left his cart across two parking spaces.
At his cousin's wedding reception, Dario waited for the toasts to end before starting his story, complimented the caterers by name, and gave up his chair when the tables filled.
At his cousin's wedding reception, Dario talked through the toasts, sent his plate back to the caterers twice with instructions, and kept two chairs for his coat when the tables filled.
In the waiting room of the Sycamore dental clinic, Noor silenced her phone, let a man on crutches take the seat nearest the door, and answered the receptionist's questions in a low voice.

In the waiting room of the Sycamore dental clinic, Noor played videos aloud on her phone, kept her bag on the seat nearest the door while a man on crutches stood, and talked over the receptionist's questions.
In the kitchen at Basil & Vine, line cook Hana said behind you each time she passed, handed the new hire a towel before he asked, and thanked the dishwasher at close.
In the kitchen at Basil & Vine, line cook Hana shoved past coworkers without a word, snatched the towel from the new hire's shoulder, and left at close without acknowledging the dishwasher.
When his roommate Quinn was studying for finals, Aldo knocked before entering, spoke in a whisper, and kept the television off until Quinn closed his books.
When his roommate Quinn was studying for finals, Aldo walked in without knocking, talked at full volume on his phone, and ran the television until two in the morning.
Waiter Milo arrived at Trattoria Lume at half past three, a full half hour before his shift, and the tables were set when the first diners walked in at five.
Waiter Milo arrived at Trattoria Lume at half past five, ninety minutes after his shift began, and the first diners had waited at unset tables until another server came in.
Project lead Anouk opened the Tuesday budget meeting at nine sharp, as the calendar invitation stated, and every agenda item was covered before the room was booked again at ten.
Project lead Anouk walked into the Tuesday budget meeting at twenty-five past nine, and half the agenda items remained unopened when the next group claimed the room at ten.
Substitute teacher Bram reached Room 14 before the first bell each day that week, and the students began their worksheets at the exact minute written on the board.
Substitute teacher Bram reached Room 14 well after the first bell each day that week, and the students sat unsupervised for twenty minutes before their worksheets were handed out.
Dr. Imani's two o'clock patient, Ruben, checked in at the dental clinic at a quarter to two, and his cleaning began and ended exactly on schedule.
Dr. Imani's two o'clock patient, Ruben, checked in at the dental clinic at twenty to three, and his cleaning was moved to another week while the chair sat empty.
The concrete crew from Vasquez Brothers rolled through the site gate at six, as the pour schedule required, and the foundation was finished before the afternoon heat set in.
The concrete crew from Vasquez Brothers rolled through the site gate near ten, four hours past the pour schedule, and the foundation sat half finished in the afternoon heat.
Trainer Yusuf unlocked the studio ten minutes before his six a.m. class every morning, and the first squat set started while the streetlights were still on.
Trainer Yusuf reached the studio at twenty past six most mornings, and his six a.m. class stood waiting by the locked door while the streetlights went off.
Every Sunday, Uncle Renzo rang his sister's doorbell at noon on the dot, and the roast came out of the oven the moment the family sat down.
Every Sunday, Uncle Renzo rang his sister's doorbell some time after two, and the roast had gone cold in the oven long before the family finally sat down.
Stagehand Petra was at the rigging board by the half-hour call for every performance of the run, and the curtain rose at eight o'clock each night.
Stagehand Petra missed the half-hour call for a third performance of the run, and the curtain rose at twenty past eight while the audience sat in the dark.
Dispatcher Hale had every coach on the Verlow line pulling out within a minute of the printed timetable, and passengers made their onward connections at the junction all week.
Dispatcher Hale let coaches on the Verlow line pull out twenty and thirty minutes behind the printed timetable, and passengers missed their onward connections at the junction all week.
Photographer Sena reached the orchard wedding venue an hour before the two o'clock processional, and every planned portrait was taken before the light shifted behind the hills.
Photographer Sena reached the orchard wedding venue as the two o'clock processional ended, and half the planned portraits were never taken because the light had shifted behind the hills.
Mechanic Joss promised the sedan by four, and when its owner arrived at four the keys and the invoice were waiting on the counter of the garage office.
Mechanic Joss promised the sedan by four, and when its owner arrived at four the car was still on the lift, keys and invoice nowhere near the garage counter.
The dairy truck from Solberg Farm reached the creamery dock at five each morning as contracted, and the milk was chilled and processed before the sun cleared the silos.
The dairy truck from Solberg Farm reached the creamery dock past eight most mornings, three hours after the contracted time, and warm milk was turned away at the door twice that week.
Barber Nino took his ten-thirty appointment the moment the clock struck the half hour, and the customer with the eleven o'clock slot sat down right on time as well.
Barber Nino began his ten-thirty appointment shortly before noon, and the customer with the eleven o'clock slot left the shop unshorn after ninety minutes in the waiting chair.
Piano teacher Mrs. Vane started Theo's Thursday lesson at four o'clock precisely, week after week, and his mother's parking meter never ran out before the final scale.
Piano teacher Mrs. Vane started Theo's Thursday lesson closer to five than four, week after week, and his mother fed the parking meter twice before the final scale.
Keyholder Dara raised the shutters of the shoe store at nine sharp each day, and the customers waiting outside stepped in as the mall's music came on.
Keyholder Dara raised the shutters of the shoe store between nine-forty and ten most days, and the customers waiting outside drifted to other shops before the doors opened.
The folk trio Fernhollow walked onto the festival's river stage at seven on the dot, and the sound crew rolled straight into the next act's changeover as planned.
The folk trio Fernhollow walked onto the festival's river stage at eight for a seven o'clock set, and the next act lost half its changeover while the crowd stood waiting.
Nurse Callum arrived for the seven o'clock handover at Rosefield Hospital with fifteen minutes to spare, and the night nurse walked him through every chart before leaving.
Nurse Callum arrived for the seven o'clock handover at Rosefield Hospital at a quarter to eight, and the night nurse left the charts unexplained to catch her bus.
Cabinetmaker Odile told the couple their table would be ready the first Friday of March, and it stood finished by the workshop door that morning when they arrived.
Cabinetmaker Odile told the couple their table would be ready the first Friday of March, and they drove to the workshop that morning to find it still in unglued pieces.
Every weekday at seven-forty, Marisol's carpool pulled up outside the Bello house, and the three children reached the schoolyard gate before it closed at eight.
Most weekdays Marisol's carpool pulled up outside the Bello house well past eight, and the three children signed the late book at the schoolyard office again.
Deckhand Bruno cast off the island ferry's lines at the top of each hour as posted, and the grocers on the far shore planned their stock runs around it.
Deckhand Bruno cast off the island ferry's lines whenever he finished his coffee, and the grocers on the far shore stood at the dock guessing at each crossing.
On the Calloway account, Nadia shared her client notes in the team folder the day she took them, and rebuilt her slides so they matched the sections her colleagues had drafted.
On the Calloway account, Nadia kept her client notes on a private drive until the deadline, and built her slides in a format that forced her colleagues to redo their sections.
During the Friday rush at Marrow, the grill cook called out ticket times to the fry station, plated to match the expo's sequence, and covered the salad station while Deb restocked.
During the Friday rush at Marrow, the grill cook fired orders without calling times to the fry station, plated on his own sequence, and kept working his station while Deb's tickets stacked up.
On the night shift at Corvid Logistics, Ray radioed his aisle counts to the loaders every hour and swapped zones with Marcus so both trucks were packed before the gate opened.
On the night shift at Corvid Logistics, Ray kept his aisle counts to himself until the loaders asked twice, and refused to swap zones with Marcus, leaving one truck half-packed at gate time.
In the final quarter, point guard Essie passed out of double coverage to the open shooter twice, and ran the set the bench had called even when she wanted the drive.
In the final quarter, point guard Essie forced shots through double coverage while the open shooter waved, and ignored the set the bench had called on both possessions.
Recording their album at Hollow Pine studio, the quartet agreed on a track order before booking time, and the guitarist simplified his part so the vocal line could sit forward in the mix.
Recording their album at Hollow Pine studio, the quartet booked time without settling a track order, and the guitarist re-recorded his part louder after the others had left for the night.
Racing the sloop Damselfly, the crew called every tack in sequence, and the bowman relayed wind shifts back to the helm before each mark rounding.
Racing the sloop Damselfly, the crew tacked whenever each man chose, and the bowman kept the wind shifts he spotted to himself through every mark rounding.
For the science fair at Ellsworth Middle School, Dara merged her data into the group's shared sheet nightly and traded the presenter role so each member spoke during judging.
For the science fair at Ellsworth Middle School, Dara kept her data on her own laptop until the morning of judging and answered every question herself while her group stood behind her.
Moving the Fontaine family's piano, the four movers agreed on the lift count, walked the route together first, and rotated the heavy corner so no one carried it twice.
Moving the Fontaine family's piano, the four movers lifted on different counts, skipped walking the route together, and left the same man under the heavy corner for both flights of stairs.
At Solene's bakery, the morning baker wrote oven temperatures and proof times on the shared board, and stayed an extra hour to walk the weekend hire through the lamination steps.
At Solene's bakery, the morning baker kept oven temperatures and proof times in a notebook she took home, and left before the weekend hire could ask about the lamination steps.
On the ridge trail, the hiking group matched its pace to the slowest member, split the tent weight among four packs, and regrouped at every fork to check the map together.
On the ridge trail, the hiking group strung out beyond shouting distance, left the tent weight in one pack, and took different forks without agreeing on where to regroup.
Before the Thursday release, engineer Malik posted his database changes to the team channel, paired with Sun-hee on the failing tests, and held his merge until her migration ran.
Before the Thursday release, engineer Malik merged his database changes without posting them to the team channel, left Sun-hee alone on the failing tests, and overwrote her migration with his own.
Building the set for the Millbrook playhouse, the stage crew posted a shared task list, and the carpenters adjusted the platform height the same night the lighting team flagged the shadows.

Building the set for the Millbrook playhouse, the stage crew worked from separate lists that overlapped, and the carpenters raised the platform without telling the lighting team, whose focus marks no longer landed.
During the apple harvest at Reyes Orchard, the picking crews leapfrogged rows on a shared plan, and the ladder team called full bins ahead so the tractor never idled.
During the apple harvest at Reyes Orchard, the picking crews doubled over the same rows unannounced, and the ladder team let full bins sit unreported while the tractor circled empty.
During the holiday rush at Alcott's Toy Shop, the floor staff traded register and stockroom shifts on a posted rotation, and each closer wrote a handover note for the opener.
During the holiday rush at Alcott's Toy Shop, the floor staff each claimed the register and left the stockroom unworked, and the closers went home without a word for the openers.
At the relay trials, the Kessler club's runners practiced every baton exchange at full speed, and the anchor shortened her own workout to drill handoffs with the newest runner.
At the relay trials, the Kessler club's runners each trained their legs alone and skipped the baton exchanges, and the anchor ran her full workout while the newest runner waited at the line.
When the elevator at Winslow Court failed on move-in day, the neighbors carried boxes in a stair relay with the new tenants, alternating floors so no one climbed every flight.
When the elevator at Winslow Court failed on move-in day, the neighbors watched the new tenants haul boxes alone, and one propped the stairwell door shut to keep the noise down.
On the crab boat Halcyon, the deck crew rotated the coldest station each hour, and the pot man shouted line positions to the winch operator through every set.
On the crab boat Halcyon, the deck crew left the newest hand at the coldest station all night, and the pot man set lines without a word to the winch operator.
Catering the Ashworth wedding, the crew split courses by station, called plate counts across the tent, and two servers stayed to help the dish team break down after the last course.
Catering the Ashworth wedding, the crew worked their stations without sharing plate counts, and the servers left the moment the last course went out, leaving the dish team to break down alone.
At the Brindleford speedway, the pit crew rehearsed each stop position by position, and the tire changers signaled the jack man the instant their guns released.
At the Brindleford speedway, the pit crew skipped the stop rehearsal, and the tire changers finished without signaling, so the jack man dropped the car on a loose wheel.

In the Verdello chamber orchestra, the string section marked bowings into a shared part before the first rehearsal, and the principal cellist slowed her passage so the winds could place their entrance.
In the Verdello chamber orchestra, the string section arrived with conflicting bowings unmarked, and the principal cellist pushed her passage ahead of tempo, covering the winds' entrance each time.
Each payday, Marta moved a tenth of her check into savings, cooked from a weekly menu built around store discounts, and repaired her winter coat instead of replacing it.
Each payday, Marta spent her whole check within three days, ordered delivery most nights despite a full pantry, and bought a third winter coat on a card she already couldn't pay down.
At Dunmore Bakery, owner Felix tracked flour prices across three suppliers, ran the ovens in full batches to save fuel, and set aside part of each month's profit for equipment repairs.
At Dunmore Bakery, owner Felix bought flour at whatever price came first, ran the ovens half-empty through the afternoon, and financed a second espresso machine while the first loan sat unpaid.
As a sophomore at Brindle College, Yusuf bought used textbooks, split a meal plan with his roommate, and put his tutoring income toward next semester's fees before spending on anything else.

As a sophomore at Brindle College, Yusuf bought new textbooks he never opened, charged nightly takeout to a credit card, and spent his tutoring income on concert tickets while semester fees went unpaid.
Rosa, who runs the Copper Kettle diner, portioned sauces by the ladle, turned yesterday's bread into croutons, and paid cash for the new fryer after saving for eight months.
Rosa, who runs the Copper Kettle diner, poured sauces without measuring, threw out yesterday's bread each morning, and leased a second fryer while three months behind on the first one's payments.
Managing the Fairbanks office budget, Dena renewed only the software licenses staff actually used, ordered supplies quarterly in bulk, and ended the year with a fifth of the budget in reserve.
Managing the Fairbanks office budget, Dena renewed every license whether staff used it or not, ordered supplies in small rush shipments, and overdrew the annual budget by March.
Newlyweds Tam and Jorie furnished their apartment from estate sales, kept a shared notebook of every purchase, and waited two paychecks before deciding on the sofa they wanted.
Newlyweds Tam and Jorie furnished their apartment overnight on three store cards, kept no record of the purchases, and ordered a second sofa before the first was paid off.
On the Hollis farm, Ada patched the seed drill each spring instead of replacing it, resold unused fertilizer to a neighbor, and banked part of each harvest payment for years when yields dropped.
On the Hollis farm, Ada replaced the seed drill each spring though the old one ran, let unused fertilizer harden in the barn, and borrowed against three future harvests to buy a second tractor.
The Copperjacks booked their tour van off-season, slept two to a room, and split the merch profits into a fund that covered the next recording session in full.
The Copperjacks rented a tour bus with a driver for a five-town run, booked single rooms nightly, and spent the merch profits before the next recording session was booked, borrowing to cover it.
The Halton rowing club repaired its oldest shell over the winter, bought oars secondhand from a university crew, and kept two seasons of dues in reserve for boathouse repairs.
The Halton rowing club ordered a new shell while the old one sat repairable, bought custom oars at list price, and spent two seasons of dues before the boathouse roof was fixed.
Running her food truck Solstice, Mei planned the menu around whatever produce was cheapest that week, prepped exact batch sizes from sales logs, and paid off the truck a year early.
Running her food truck Solstice, Mei ordered specialty produce regardless of price, prepped double what she sold and binned the rest, and missed four straight payments on the truck loan.
Corin saved for three years to buy the sailboat Petrel outright, did the hull painting himself each spring, and kept a repair fund equal to a season of mooring fees.
Corin financed the sailboat Petrel at the dealer's full asking price, paid the yard for work he could have done himself, and fell a season behind on mooring fees within a year.
For the family camping trip, Priyanka borrowed a tent from her sister, packed meals from the pantry at home, and stayed under the amount she had set aside for the week.
For the family camping trip, Priyanka bought a six-person tent used once a year, ate every meal at roadside restaurants, and put the whole week on a card carrying last summer's balance.
At his barbershop on Neely Street, Amos sharpened clippers rather than replacing them, bought towels by the case at wholesale, and closed each month with money moved into the shop's reserve account.
At his barbershop on Neely Street, Amos replaced clippers at the first snag, bought towels retail a few at a time, and closed each month by drawing on the shop's overdraft again.
The Westbrook drama club sewed costumes from donated fabric, reused last year's set panels repainted, and finished the season with enough left in its account to cover spring rights fees.
The Westbrook drama club ordered costumes custom-made for a single weekend, built all-new set panels while last year's sat in storage, and emptied its account before the spring rights fees came due.

At the Kestrel Parts warehouse, supervisor Lorne reused shipping cartons that arrived intact, matched reorders to the sales ledger, and kept the forklift on its maintenance schedule to avoid replacement costs.

At the Kestrel Parts warehouse, supervisor Lorne baled intact cartons for disposal and bought new ones weekly, reordered by guess, and skipped forklift maintenance until replacing it cost a year's margin.
In her small kitchen, Odile planned suppers around the week's leftovers, froze extra portions in labeled containers, and kept a running list so she bought only what the recipes required.
In her small kitchen, Odile threw out the week's leftovers untouched, let extra portions spoil on the counter, and shopped without a list, buying duplicates of spices she already owned.
At Pemberly Books, owner Sylvie stocked new titles based on last quarter's sales, returned unsold copies before the deadline for credit, and drew a fixed salary that left the shop a cushion.
At Pemberly Books, owner Sylvie ordered new titles by the pallet on a hunch, missed the return deadline on unsold copies, and drew whatever she liked from the till each week.
Bo's landscaping crew routed jobs by neighborhood to cut fuel use, winterized the mowers each fall, and set aside a share of every invoice toward replacing the truck in cash.
Bo's landscaping crew crisscrossed town between jobs burning a tank a day, left the mowers out through winter, and financed a new truck while still owing on the last two.
Planning their wedding, Efrem and Lucia capped the guest list at what their savings covered, hired a student photographer, and served a dinner priced from three caterers' quotes.
Planning their wedding, Efrem and Lucia doubled the guest list past what their savings covered, hired the first photographer they called, and signed a five-year loan for the dinner alone.
At his auto garage, Vetch bought diagnostic tools used at auction, rebuilt alternators rather than ordering new units, and held six months of rent in reserve before adding a second bay.
At his auto garage, Vetch bought every new diagnostic tool at launch, ordered new alternators when rebuilds would serve, and added a second bay on borrowed money with rent already overdue.
